2606.09234.title: End-to-End Training for Discrete Token LLM based TTS System
2606.09234.paper_summary: Recent state-of-the-art (SOTA) text-to-speech (TTS) systems typically adopt a cascaded pipeline consisting of a speech tokenizer, an autoregressive large language model (LLM), and a diffusion based flow-matching (FM) model, with these components trained independently. In this paper, we propose a fully end-to-end (E2E) optimization framework that unifies the training of the speech tokenizer, LLM, FM model, and an additional reward model (RM). Specifically, we first jointly optimize the tokenizer using multi-task objectives derived from reconstruction for FM, next-token prediction for LLM, and multi recognition task for RM. This joint training encourages the discrete speech token space to capture acoustically and semantically salient information that is better tailored to TTS. We then further optimize the LLM using downstream reconstruction and recognition by FM and RM, which reduces inference-time mismatch and steers the LLM toward more preferred generations. Experimental results show that our E2E framework consistently outperforms cascaded baselines. On the Seed-TTS-Eval benchmark, our system achieves a word error rate (WER) of 0.78% and 1.56%, a new SOTA result with a 0.6B-parameter LLM and 0.5B-parameter FM model. These results validate that holistic E2E optimization is critical for improving discrete-token-based TTS systems with a much simpler training pipeline.
2606.09234.paper_summary_zh: 最近的最先進（SOTA）文本到語音（TTS）系統通常採用一個級聯管道，該管道由語音標記器、自回歸大型語言模型（LLM）和基於擴散的流匹配（FM）模型組成，這些組件是獨立訓練的。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一個完全端到端（E2E）優化框架，統一了語音標記器、LLM、FM模型和額外的獎勵模型（RM）的訓練。<lb/>具體而言，我們首先使用從FM重建、LLM的下一個標記預測和RM的多重識別任務衍生的多任務目標來共同優化標記器。<lb/>這種聯合訓練促使離散語音標記空間捕捉聲學和語義上重要的信息，這些信息更適合於TTS。<lb/>然後，我們進一步使用FM和RM的下游重建和識別來優化LLM，這減少了推理時間的不匹配，並引導LLM朝向更受偏好的生成。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，我們的E2E框架在各級聯基準上始終表現優於。<lb/>在Seed-TTS-Eval基準上，我們的系統達到了0.78%和1.56%的字錯誤率（WER），這是使用0.6B參數的LLM和0.5B參數的FM模型所取得的新SOTA結果。<lb/>這些結果驗證了整體E2E優化對於改善基於離散標記的TTS系統至關重要，並且具有更簡單的訓練管道。
2606.09234.author: Changfeng Gao et.al.
2606.09234.authors: Changfeng Gao, Yong Ren, Jun Yuan, Ye Bai, Zhao You, ShiDong Shang
